text: While the accuracy of contemporary parsing models is greatly improved, parsing still remains a performance bottleneck. Most dependency parsers use millions of sparse indicator features, which leads to a performance degradation due to poor generalization and a large amount of feature extractions. To efficiently and effectively disambiguate the actions in transition-based dependency parsing, we develop a new neural network classifier named ‘PT-Graph’ which learns a compact and powerful set of representations as parsing progresses, and utilising these representations in a discriminative manner. The proposed parser achieved, on average, an improvement of 2% (2.5 points) in labeled and unlabeled attachment scores for both English and Chinese parse trees while maintaining an average parsing speed of over 1,000 sentences per second with a parsing accuracy of 92.2% (UAS) on the English Penn Treebank.
